title: Fix type-parameterized interface as a port parameter
body:
When creating an instance of a module with a parametrized interface port and passing it a hierarchical reference to such an interface, Verilator issues an error, as it seem not to be able to deduce the right parameter value.

The following example has a type parameter:

```SystemVerilog
interface A #(parameter type TYPE);
endinterface

module B#(parameter type TYPE);
  A #(TYPE) a();
endmodule

module C(A a);
endmodule

module test;
  B #(logic) b();
  C c(b.a);
endmodule
```

Errors::

```
%Error: bug.sv:8:10: Missing type parameter: 'TYPE'
                   : ... note: In instance 'test.c'
    8 | module C(A a);
      |          ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

The following code has a non-type parameter:

```SystemVerilog
interface A #(parameter W);
endinterface

module B#(parameter W);
  A #(8) a();
endmodule

module C(A a);
endmodule

module test;
  B #(8) b();
  C c(b.a); // error 
endmodule
```

Slightly different errors:

```
%Error: bug.sv:1:25: Parameter without initial value is never given value (IEEE 1800-2023 6.20.1): 'W'
  : ... note: In instance 'test.b'
1 | interface A #(parameter W);
|                         ^
%Error: bug.sv:8:10: Parent instance's interface is not found: 'A'
  : ... note: In instance 'test.c'
8 | module C(A a);
|          ^
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
```

To be honest, I wasn't really able to determine from the standard whether this is legal, but Vivado, VCS, and Questa all accept it without any problems.

> What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

`verilator --timing --trace --top-module test--binary bug.sv`

> What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

Verilator 5.030.

> What OS and distribution are you using?

Ubuntu 22.04 under WSL.

> May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

If this look like someone who is a Verilator beginner could reasonably handle.
